mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 60-day forward windows or fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline by more than 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply chain bottlenecks that trigger short-term equity sell-offs within the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with industrial production declines during supply-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 4.0 percent forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in over 55% of months where the CPI threshold is breached, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands consumer spending capacity and corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day forward returns exhibit a negative or zero correlation with payroll growth over any consecutive 24-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates demand fragility that precedes equity market corrections within the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to produce a statistically significant negative coefficient in out-of-sample regression tests over 20-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid acceleration in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals capacity utilization peaks that trigger short-term profit-taking and negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where industrial production accelerates during tightening periods.
